title: Time Bookings — Clock In / Clock Out

questions.prompt: What exactly are "time bookings"? How do they differ from the existing shift clock-in/clock-out?
questions.description: PAL already has shift-based clock-in/clock-out (scheduled → confirmed → completed with clocked_in_at/clocked_out_at on the shifts table). Is this about: enhancing the existing shift clocking, building a separate general time-tracking system (like timesheets independent of shifts), or something else entirely?
questions.recommendedAnswer: Separate general time-tracking system — staff clock in/out for their overall work hours, independent of specific shifts/events.

options.label: Enhance existing shift clocking (e.g. better UX, multiple clock cycles per shift, break tracking)
Separate time booking system — general work-hour logging, not tied to shifts
Both — keep shift clocking AND add a separate time booking layer
Something else — I'll describe it below

questions.description: Which user roles should be able to use time bookings?
questions.recommendedAnswer: All staff members can clock in/out for themselves. Managers/admins can view everyone's time bookings.

options.label: Staff clock in/out for themselves
Staff + managers clock for themselves
Managers can clock in/out other staff on their behalf
Managers/admins can view and manage everyone's time bookings
Also expose via API for integrations (e.g. door system auto-clock-in)

questions.prompt: What level of granularity do you need?
questions.description: How detailed should each time booking be?
questions.recommendedAnswer: Simple clock in / clock out with optional break pauses. One active booking at a time per user.

options.label: Just clock in and clock out — one entry per work session
Clock in, pause (breaks), resume, clock out — tracks total worked time minus breaks
Clock in/out per activity or task (e.g. 'setup', 'service', 'cleanup')
Also track WHERE they clocked in (venue, zone, sub-location)

questions.prompt: Should time bookings be linked to shifts, events, or venues?
questions.description: When someone clocks in, should the booking be associated with a specific shift/event/venue, or is it free-form?
questions.recommendedAnswer: Optional linkage — when a staff member has a scheduled shift for that day, auto-suggest linking. Otherwise free-form.

options.label: Auto-link to the active shift if one exists
Optional — let the user pick a shift/event/venue or leave it unlinked
Always link to a venue (which venue am I at?)
No linkage needed — just start/stop times

questions.prompt: Should users be able to have multiple active time bookings?
questions.description: E.g. can someone be clocked in at two venues simultaneously, or should the system enforce one active booking at a time?
questions.recommendedAnswer: Enforce one active booking — clocking in somewhere new should auto-clock-out from the previous one, or block it.

options.label: Only one active at a time — clocking in elsewhere auto-clocks-out the previous
Allow multiple but warn the user
Allow multiple simultaneous bookings without restriction
Block clock-in if already clocked in somewhere

questions.prompt: Can time bookings be edited or corrected after the fact?
questions.description: What if someone forgets to clock in/out, or clocks in at the wrong time?
questions.recommendedAnswer: Staff can request a correction, managers/admins can approve and edit time bookings. All edits are logged.

options.label: Managers/admins can edit or add time bookings retroactively
Staff can edit their own bookings within a time window (e.g. 24h)
Staff can request a correction that needs manager approval
All edits are logged (audit trail) so changes are visible
No editing allowed — what's recorded is final

questions.prompt: What do you want to do with the time booking data?
questions.description: How will the recorded time be used downstream?
questions.recommendedAnswer: Overview dashboard showing who's currently clocked in, history per staff member, and total hours for payroll/export.

options.label: Live dashboard — see who's currently clocked in right now
Per-staff history — see my past time bookings
Total hours summary — per day/week/month per staff
Export for payroll/accounting (CSV, PDF)
Integration with payroll — hours feed into compensation calculations
Alerts — notify managers if someone hasn't clocked out, worked too long, etc.

questions.prompt: Should there be manual time entry (not real-time clocking)?
questions.description: E.g. a manager adding a past time booking for someone who forgot their phone or the system was down.
questions.recommendedAnswer: Yes, managers should be able to manually add time bookings with a reason field.

options.label: Yes — manual entry with reason/notes
No — only real-time clock in/out

questions.recommendedAnswer: Web app (mobile-friendly) as the primary interface. Future: QR code scan at venue, NFC, or door system integration.

options.label: Web app on desktop
Web app on mobile (responsive)
QR code scan at venue (scan a code to clock in/out)
NFC tap (physical card/phone tap)
Geofencing — auto-clock-in when entering venue area
Door system integration — clock in automatically when badge is scanned at door

questions.prompt: What's the most important part to build first?
questions.description: If we can't do everything at once, what's the MVP?
questions.recommendedAnswer: Basic clock in/out with a live dashboard showing who's clocked in.

options.label: Basic clock in/out + live 'who's here' dashboard
Personal time booking history for each staff member
Admin view — all time bookings, editing, export
QR-based clock in/out at venues
